title: salix rosmarinifolia subsp. rosmarinifolia
intro: Salix rosmarinifolia subsp. rosmarinifolia, also known as Rosemary Willow, is a subspecies of willow tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rosemary Willow include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and improve overall well-being. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
